event: Archaic hunter-gatherer peoples are believed to have first reached Jamaica from neighboring Caribbean islands.
Redware-pottery Ostionoid (pre-Taino) peoples migrate to Jamaica, establishing the island's earliest ceramic-using settlements.
Taino (Arawak) people, who called the island Xaymaca ('land of wood and water'), settle Jamaica and develop agricultural village societies.
Christopher Columbus lands on Jamaica during his second voyage, claiming the island for Spain.
Columbus is marooned and shipwrecked off Jamaica for about a year during his fourth voyage before being rescued.
Spain begins formal colonization of Jamaica, with Juan de Esquivel establishing the first settlement at Sevilla la Nueva.
The Spanish move their capital inland to Villa de la Vega, later known as Spanish Town.
An English expedition under Penn and Venables captures Jamaica from Spain during Oliver Cromwell's Western Design.
Spanish forces are finally expelled, and freed and escaped enslaved Africans form the early Maroon communities in the interior.
The Treaty of Madrid formally cedes Jamaica to England, ending Spanish claims to the island.
A massive earthquake destroys much of Port Royal, killing thousands and sinking part of the pirate-era city into the sea.
Britain signs a peace treaty with the Leeward Maroons under Cudjoe, granting them autonomy after the First Maroon War.
Tacky's Revolt, a major uprising of enslaved Africans, erupts in St. Mary parish and spreads across the island.
The Second Maroon War breaks out between the Trelawny Town Maroons and British colonial authorities.
Britain abolishes the transatlantic slave trade, ending the legal importation of enslaved Africans to Jamaica.
The Baptist War (Christmas Rebellion), a large slave uprising led by Samuel Sharpe, is brutally suppressed.
Slavery is formally abolished under the Slavery Abolition Act, replaced temporarily by an apprenticeship system.
Full emancipation is achieved as the apprenticeship system ends, freeing roughly 300,000 people.
Indentured laborers from India begin arriving to work on plantations following emancipation.
The Morant Bay Rebellion, led by Paul Bogle, is violently crushed, prompting Jamaica to become a Crown Colony.
Kingston replaces Spanish Town as the capital of Jamaica.
A devastating earthquake and fire strike Kingston, killing around 1,000 people and destroying much of the city.
Marcus Garvey founds the Universal Negro Improvement Association, launching a global Black nationalist movement.
The Rastafari movement emerges, inspired by Garveyism and the coronation of Haile Selassie I in Ethiopia.
Island-wide labor riots lead to the founding of trade unions and the People's National Party under Norman Manley.
The Jamaica Labour Party is founded by Alexander Bustamante, establishing the island's two-party political system.
A new constitution introduces universal adult suffrage and Jamaica's first elections under it.
Jamaica joins the West Indies Federation, a short-lived union of British Caribbean colonies.
Jamaican voters reject continued membership in the West Indies Federation in a national referendum.
Jamaica gains full independence from the United Kingdom on August 6 and joins the United Nations.
Michael Manley becomes prime minister, ushering in an era of democratic socialist policies.
Reggae star Bob Marley survives an assassination attempt amid intense political violence before an election.
Bob Marley headlines the One Love Peace Concert, uniting rival political leaders Michael Manley and Edward Seaga on stage.
Edward Seaga's JLP wins a landslide election after a year of severe political violence that killed hundreds.
Reggae icon Bob Marley dies of cancer, cementing his global cultural legacy.
Hurricane Gilbert devastates Jamaica, causing widespread destruction and billions in damage.
P. J. Patterson becomes Jamaica's first prime minister of fully African descent.
Hurricane Ivan causes severe damage across the island, killing several people and displacing thousands.
Portia Simpson-Miller becomes Jamaica's first female prime minister.
Sprinter Usain Bolt sets world records and wins multiple golds at the Beijing Olympics, becoming a national icon.
A state of emergency and deadly security operation in Tivoli Gardens targets drug lord Christopher 'Dudus' Coke.
Andrew Holness becomes prime minister after the JLP wins a narrow general election victory.
Hurricane Beryl strikes Jamaica as a powerful storm, causing significant damage along the coast.
